growth_records.cite: Midani et al. AMiGA: Software for Automated Analysis of Microbial Growth Assays. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00508-21. [PMC8409736]
growth_records.snippet: its growth rates on these substrates were different (95% confidence intervals are 0.57 to 0.64 h−1 and 0.33 to 0.48 h−1, respectively)
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate (r) 95% CI reported for CD2015 on fructose as 0.57-0.64 h-1 from Gaussian-process regression model fit to Biolog PM1 growth curves; midpoint 0.605 h-1 taken as point estimate. doubling_time_h=ln2/0.605=1.15 h derived. Conditions: anaerobic atmosphere (5% CO2, 5% H2, 90% N2), 37°C, prereduced minimal medium (Methods).

growth_records.medium: minimal defined medium + trehalose (Biolog PM1 plate well)
growth_records.cite: Midani et al. AMiGA: Software for Automated Analysis of Microbial Growth Assays. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00508-21. [PMC8409736]
growth_records.snippet: its growth rates on these substrates were different (95% confidence intervals are 0.57 to 0.64 h−1 and 0.33 to 0.48 h−1, respectively)
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate (r) 95% CI reported for CD2015 on trehalose as 0.33-0.48 h-1 from Gaussian-process regression model fit to Biolog PM1 growth curves; midpoint 0.405 h-1 taken as point estimate. doubling_time_h=ln2/0.405=1.71 h derived. Conditions: anaerobic atmosphere (5% CO2, 5% H2, 90% N2), 37°C, prereduced minimal medium (Methods).

growth_records.strain: ATCC 43255 ΔPaLoc (PaLoc-deleted, non-toxigenic; ΔtcdB ΔtcdE ΔtcdA)
growth_records.medium: Modified minimal medium (MMM) = C. difficile minimal medium (CDMM) + selenite + 13C-glucose

up.method: HRMAS 13C NMR logistic-fit concentration trajectory over 36 h (estimated concentration from 13C peak area scaled by standards + NOE correction)

sec.method: HRMAS 13C NMR; [13C]acetate first detected at 7 h
HRMAS 13C NMR; [13C]alanine first detected at 10 h; 1:1 [2,3-13C]/[U-13C]alanine indicating 12CO2 assimilation via PFOR
HRMAS 13C NMR; n-[13C]butanol detected at 35 h

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Exchange fluxes for all NMR-detected metabolites (glucose uptake; acetate/ethanol/lactate/butyrate/butanol secretion)
mfa.note: dFBA exchange bounds constrained to 95% CI of the logistic derivative (eq.2) of experimental 13C NMR trajectories; numeric per-timepoint values in Supplementary Tables 3 & 5 (not itemized in main text). Model = icdf843 (update of icdf834), objective ATP hydrolysis.

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Pyruvate:ferredoxin oxidoreductase (PFOR) / acetate kinase / PGK / pyruvate kinase (oxidative glycolytic + mixed-acid)
mfa.note: dFBA-PREDICTED (Fig 3c), FVA upper bound of acetate kinase reached 1,000 mM/h. Model-inferred, not directly measured.

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Alanine transaminase (pyruvate->alanine) as glycolysis-Stickland N integration point
mfa.note: dFBA-PREDICTED (Fig 3i); FVA bounds ±1,000 mM/h. Leucine-origin ammonia (via glutamate) supplies 56.4% of alanine amino nitrogen (model); experimentally confirmed by 15N-13C J-coupling: 57%±4% of [13C]alanine carried 15N though [15N]leucine was only 33% of media amino-N.

product_yields.alanine_15N_from_leucine: 57%±4% of 13C-alanine carried 15N vs 33% 15N in media amino-N (experimental, 15N-13C J-coupling)

growth_records.cite: Pavao, Aidan et al. Elucidating dynamic anaerobe metabolism with HRMAS 13C NMR and genome-scale modeling. Nature Chemical Biology (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41589-023-01275-9. [PMC10154198]
growth_records.snippet: HRMAS NMR of C. difficile grown with [U-13C]glucose identified [13C]acetate at 7 h and [13C]alanine at 10 h ... with production of [13C]ethanol, followed by [13C]lactate at 21 h, [13C]butyrate at 24 h and n-[13C]butanol at 35 h. MMM ... sup
growth_records.notes: Glucose condition. Substrate [U-13C]glucose 27.5 mM (consistent with stated 0.5% w/v glucose: 5 g/L / 180.16 g/mol = 27.75 mM). Substrate NOT fully consumed by 36 h (logistic constant C = residual substrate), so no delta-substrate/time uptake rate computed. Product onset times are explicit; per-timepoint estimated concentrations (mM) and derived exchange fluxes (mM/h) reside in Supplementary Tables 2-5, not in article text, hence rate=null. Rates are volumetric (mM/h); cellular biomass not quantified in gDW (~100,000 CFU inoculum), so mmol/gDW/h not derivable. No cellular growth rate/doubling time reported in text (growth data in Supplementary Table 1). CDMM full composition is by reference (ref 41), not itemized here.

growth_records.medium: Modified minimal medium (MMM) with L-[U-13C]proline as labeled substrate

up.method: Derived average: complete proline consumption within 5 h from 7.0 mM starting concentration (HRMAS 13C NMR trajectory)

sec.method: HRMAS 13C NMR; [U-13C]5-aminovalerate produced (proline reductase, reductive Stickland; single product of proline reduction)

mfa.note: dFBA reaction 13 (Fig 3e); exchange fluxes NMR-constrained. Selenium-dependent selenoenzyme coupled to Rnf/ATP synthesis. Numeric values in Supplementary Tables.

growth_records.cite: Pavao, Aidan et al. Elucidating dynamic anaerobe metabolism with HRMAS 13C NMR and genome-scale modeling. Nature Chemical Biology (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41589-023-01275-9. [PMC10154198]
growth_records.snippet: HRMAS NMR of C. difficile cultures growing in media containing L-[U-13C]proline revealed complete proline consumption within 5 hours (h), producing [U-13C]5-aminovalerate ... growth in MMM with 7.0 mM [U-13C]proline
growth_records.notes: Proline condition. qS(avg) = delta-substrate/time = 7.0 mM / 5 h = 1.40 mM/h (explicit: complete consumption within 5 h). Volumetric rate (mM/h); no gDW biomass so mmol/gDW/h not derivable. Proline reductase yields a single product (5-aminovalerate). No cellular growth rate reported in text.

growth_records.medium: Modified minimal medium (MMM) with L-[U-13C]leucine as labeled substrate

up.method: Derived average: complete leucine metabolism within 13 h from 7.6 mM starting concentration (HRMAS 13C NMR trajectory)

sec.method: HRMAS 13C NMR; [13C]isovalerate level rose over 2.7-8.7 h (oxidative leucine fermentation; also yields CO2, ferredoxin, ATP)
HRMAS 13C NMR; [13C]isocaproate level rose over 7.4-13.0 h (reductive leucine fermentation)
Stoichiometric product of oxidative leucine->isovalerate fermentation (not separately NMR-quantified)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Oxidative leucine fermentation (isovalerate kinase, reaction 1) and reductive leucine (isocaprenoyl-CoA reductase, reaction 12)
mfa.note: dFBA-predicted (Fig 3a,e); oxidative branch yields 2 ferredoxin + 1 ATP, reductive branch (isocaproate) consumes 4 net reducing equivalents. Exchange fluxes NMR-constrained; numeric values in Supplementary Tables.

growth_records.cite: Pavao, Aidan et al. Elucidating dynamic anaerobe metabolism with HRMAS 13C NMR and genome-scale modeling. Nature Chemical Biology (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41589-023-01275-9. [PMC10154198]
growth_records.snippet: HRMAS NMR revealed complete metabolism of L-[U-13C]leucine within 13 h, with levels of [13C]isovalerate and [13C]isocaproate rising over 2.7-8.7 and 7.4-13.0 h, respectively ... growth in MMM with 7.6 mM [U-13C]leucine
growth_records.notes: Leucine condition. qS(avg) = delta-substrate/time = 7.6 mM / 13 h = 0.585 mM/h (explicit: complete metabolism within 13 h). Leucine split between oxidative (->isovalerate + CO2) and reductive (->isocaproate) Stickland pathways. Volumetric rate; gDW not available. No cellular growth rate reported in text.

growth_records.medium: MMM + 30 mM L-[U-13C]proline WITH 100 µM sodium selenite (selenium-replete)

up.method: HRMAS 13C NMR; proline depletion estimated as ratio of final/starting 13C-proline signal (excess 30 mM)

sec.method: HRMAS 13C NMR; robust [U-13C]5-aminovalerate production with selenite present (proline reductase is a selenoenzyme)

growth_records.cite: Pavao, Aidan et al. Elucidating dynamic anaerobe metabolism with HRMAS 13C NMR and genome-scale modeling. Nature Chemical Biology (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41589-023-01275-9. [PMC10154198]
growth_records.snippet: we next compared C. difficile's proline reductase metabolism, a selenium-dependent pathway, in the presence of an excess of 30 mM [U-13C]proline ... C. difficile grown in MMM with 100 µM sodium selenite
growth_records.notes: Selenium-replete perturbation control (30 mM proline + 100 µM selenite). Serves as the 100% reference for the selenium-deficient condition (companion record). Rates not numerically given (relative signal ratios only). No cellular growth rate in text.

growth_records.medium: MMM + 30 mM L-[U-13C]proline WITHOUT sodium selenite (selenium-deficient)

up.method: HRMAS 13C NMR; proline reductase impaired without selenium

sec.method: HRMAS 13C NMR; selenium-deficient cells produced only 7% as much [U-13C]5-aminovalerate signal as selenium-replete cells

growth_records.cite: Pavao, Aidan et al. Elucidating dynamic anaerobe metabolism with HRMAS 13C NMR and genome-scale modeling. Nature Chemical Biology (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41589-023-01275-9. [PMC10154198]
growth_records.snippet: Compared to C. difficile grown in MMM with 100 µM sodium selenite, cells grown in selenium-deficient MMM produced only 7% as much [U-13C]5-aminovalerate signal
growth_records.notes: Selenium-deficient perturbation (30 mM proline, no selenite). Key experimental result: 5-aminovalerate production drops to 7% of selenium-replete level, confirming selenium dependence of proline reductase selenoenzyme. Rates relative (signal ratio), not absolute. No cellular growth rate in text.
